title: US-Iran war news LIVE: US warned Iran of Israel's plan to kill Iranian negotiators as Washington tried to pursue a deal
content:
• Iran's joint military command on Thursday (local time) warned that all oil tankers transiting through the Strait of Hormuz must use approved routes or face a “forceful response.”
• Former Supreme Leader of Iran, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was killed by US and Israeli airstrikes in late February, with his funeral events beginning over the weekend in Tehran.
• Brent crude oil traded above $71 a barrel, and West Texas Intermediate was near $68, reflecting market movements amid US-Iran talks.

title: World News Today Live Updates on July 3, 2026 : Russia launches largest-ever drone, missile barrage on Ukraine's Kyiv, at least 27 killed, 91 injured: Details
content:
• On July 3, 2026, Russia launched its largest-ever drone and missile barrage on Ukraine's Kyiv, resulting in at least 27 fatalities and 91 injuries.
• Livemint's Personal Finance coverage, relevant for Banking/SSC exams, includes insights into mutual funds, small savings schemes like PPF and SSY, post office savings schemes, top bank FDs, personal loans, credit cards, real estate, and income tax.
• Livemint's Economy coverage, crucial for Banking/SSC exams, provides analysis of India's macro and micro-economic indicators such as GDP, inflation, forex, fiscal deficit, current account deficit, interest rate cycle, RBI circulars, GST, and Insolvency and Bankruptcy.

title: Russia launches largest-ever drone, missile barrage on Ukraine's Kyiv, at least 27 killed, 91 injured: Details
content:
• The latest Russian drone and missile barrage on Ukraine's Kyiv on Thursday resulted in 27 people killed and at least 91 injured.
• Following the attack, the Red Cross' Ukrainian branch reported a loss of humanitarian aid worth around $2 million due to its key warehouse being destroyed.
• Ukraine's air force stated that Moscow launched at least 496 drones and 74 missiles in the latest barrage, with 476 drones and 48 missiles intercepted.

title: What is ‘birth tourism’? Trump administration's new target after Supreme Court backs birthright citizenship
content:
• The US Justice Department is now targeting 'birth tourism schemes', with official Colin McDonald warning that individuals using "false pretenses" to give birth in the US could face criminal charges under laws barring visa fraud, money laundering, identity theft, and wire fraud.
• The Supreme Court, in a 6-3 ruling, struck down a Trump executive order that sought to restrict birthright citizenship, which would have affected an estimated 250,000 children born annually to undocumented immigrants or temporary residents.
• The US Embassy in India stated in 2025 that US consular officers will deny tourist visa applications if the primary purpose of travel is to give birth in the United States to obtain US citizenship for the child.

title: ISRO headquarters receives hoax bomb threat via email, security forces launch search operation
content:
• Delhi Police apprehended Nishant Tyagi, a 36-year-old man from Ghaziabad, on June 30 for allegedly sending a series of hoax bomb threat emails.
• The hoax bomb threat emails were sent on June 29, targeting key government organizations including ISRO, NIA, DRDO, NPCIL, MoCA, and an Air India flight.
• The arrested individual, Nishant Tyagi, has reportedly been undergoing treatment for a mental illness since 2008.

title: India-UK trade pact sparks rush of export orders ahead of July 15 rollout
content:
• The India-UK trade pact (CETA) is scheduled to be rolled out on July 15.
• Under the CETA agreement, tariffs of up to 16% on leather and footwear, up to 12% on textiles and clothing, and up to 8% on chemicals and pharmaceuticals will be eliminated.
• Gems and jewellery exports to the UK are projected to reach around $2.5 billion, with bilateral trade potentially touching $7 billion under the new agreement.

title: RBI backs crypto containment and keeps ban on table, officials tell house panel
content:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) recommended that banks and regulated financial institutions should not be permitted to hold, trade, or undertake exposure to crypto assets or privately issued stablecoins.
• The RBI stated that "prohibition remains one of the recognised policy options" under international frameworks for virtual digital assets and continues to merit careful consideration.
• The RBI warned that widespread adoption of stablecoins could undermine India's monetary sovereignty, weaken monetary policy transmission, and fragment payment systems, posing broader financial stability risks.

title: India-EU FTA legal scrub to end in 10-12 days, deal by Dec 31: Piyush Goyal
content:
• The legal scrub for the India-EU Free Trade Agreement (FTA) is expected to conclude in 10-12 days, with the deal targeted for approval by December 31, as stated by Piyush Goyal.
• India's exports are likely to grow 15% in the June quarter from a year ago, according to Minister Piyush Goyal.
• India demands a competitive edge and preferential access to the US market under the proposed interim trade deal, a position acknowledged by US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer.

title: RBI tells parliamentary panel that virtual digital assets like cryptocurrency a threat to economy
content:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) informed the Parliamentary Standing Committee on Finance that virtual digital assets (VDAs) like cryptocurrency are a threat to an emerging economy like India and should not be legalised.
• The Parliamentary Standing Committee on Finance, headed by BJP member Bhartruhari Mahtab, held a meeting with the RBI and the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) on 'A Study on Virtual Digital Assets (VDAs) and Way Forward'.
• The RBI highlighted that countries like China and Qatar have banned virtual digital assets, whereas Europe has allowed them in a stringent, regulated manner.

title: Govt fast-tracks disinvestment process to shore up revenues, garners 31% of budget aim in Q1 FY27
content:
• The government has raised Rs 24,928 crore from disinvestment and asset monetisation in the first quarter of FY27, which is 31% of its full-year budgeted target of Rs 80,000 crore for FY27.
• During Q1 FY27, Rs 18,561 crore was garnered from Offer for Sale (OFS) of six public sector enterprises: Central Bank of India, Coal India, NHPC, NLC India, GIC, and IRFC, with an additional Rs 6,367 crore from asset monetisation under the Infrastructure Investment Trust (InvIT).
• Fixing separate disinvestment targets has been discontinued since the Revised Estimate (RE) of FY2023-24; now, an estimated Rs 80,000 crore is kept under Miscellaneous Capital Receipts for BE 2026-27 (FY27). The government aims to reduce its 96.5% stake in LIC to 90% by May next year and proceed with the strategic sale of IDBI Bank.
